title: platanthera psycodes f. ecalcarata
intro: Platanthera psycodes f. ecalcarata, also known as the "hairy orchid," is a rare and endangered subspecies of orchid native to North America. The hairy orchid has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the hairy orchid is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its availability is extremely limited due to its rarity and endangered status.
